body.title: Fascia graceful degradation: cached screenshot tier
body.result: screenshot_capture.py (Chrome headless), /api/device/{id}/screenshot endpoint, loadFascia offline detection + greyed cached screenshot overlay, Nanny hourly sweep — 10 tests
body.intention: I intend that offline devices show a greyed cached screenshot of their last-known fascia state rather than a bare 404.
body.description:
When a device tab is clicked and the device is offline, show a greyed cached screenshot of the last-known fascia page rather than the empty 404 stub. Requires periodic headless screenshot capture (playwright or similar) + storage per device + staleness detection.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (new /api/device/{id}/screenshot endpoint), new screenshot capture script
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; screenshots stored as flat files under datacenter_logs/web_server/screenshots/
**Scope boundary:** Screenshot capture + serve + greyed overlay. NOT the live 4-box fascia (that's T-device-fascia-page).
**Completion criteria:** Offline device tab shows greyed screenshot of last-known fascia; online device tab shows live fascia.
**Validation:** Stop Igor, reload page, click Igor tab — greyed screenshot appears.
